[vc_row][vc_column][vc_empty_space height=”50px” el_class=”height30″][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row el_class=”flexrow”][vc_column width=”1/4″ el_class=”flextwo gt-custom-sidebar-column”][vc_widget_sidebar sidebar_id=”honeymoon-package-indian-sidebar”][/vc_column][vc_column width=”3/4″ el_class=”second-div-text flexone”][vc_custom_heading text=”Budget-Friendly Manali Honeymoon Packages at ATW Travels”][vc_column_text]

Planning the perfect honeymoon is not an easy task. At ATW Travels, we’ve put together fantastic Manali honeymoon packages that match your taste and budget. Our expert travel planners take care of everything from transport arrangements and accommodation to sightseeing and food. Call us at 9693339999 or fill in the contact form for package details.

[/vc_column_text][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_custom_heading text=”Why Is Manali an Ideal Honeymoon Destination?”][vc_column_text]

Manali isn’t only about snow-capped mountains and unlimited adventure opportunities. The place is full of hidden gems like there is a lot of hidden gems like Patilkuhl, Thanedar, and Soyal. If you’d like to combine sightseeing with adventure activities like hiking, mountaineering, or rafting, Manali is the perfect place for a honeymoon. At ATW Travels, we have partnered with several trade associations and hotels to provide some of the best Manali packages for couples.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_single_image image=”19385″ img_size=”full” alignment=”center”][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_custom_heading text=”Tips For a Stress-Free Honeymoon in Manali” font_container=”tag:h3|text_align:left”][vc_column_text]

  • Pack your clothing depending on the season and location. Some places experience sub-zero temperatures during the winter, while temperature ranges from 5 to 15 degrees even in summer in places like Kinnaur and Chamba.
  • The rainy season is the best time to shop for low Manali honeymoon package prices, but make sure you check for landslides and other problems before booking.
  • Keep enough cash if you’re visiting a remote location
  • Don’t forget to carry a government-issued ID along with a negative RT- PCR test or vaccination certificate.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_custom_heading text=”How Much Does a Manali Honeymoon Package Cost?” font_container=”tag:h3|text_align:left”][vc_column_text]

At ATW Travels, we typically suggest an 8nights and 9days romantic Manali honeymoon tour package for clients looking for a mid-range tour package. The price ranges anywhere from INR 4,250 to INR 48,349, depending on the package chosen and add-ons provided.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_custom_heading text=”What Are the Best Places to Visit for Honeymoon Couples?” font_container=”tag:h3|text_align:left”][vc_column_text]

With its fantastic scenery and romantic atmosphere, Manali is one of the best romantic destinations in India. Some of the famous places we include in our Manali honeymoon packages include:

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner full_width=”stretch_row_content_no_spaces” css=”.vc_custom_1645675083319{margin-bottom: 20px !important;padding-top: 40px !important;padding-bottom: 40px !important;background-color: #1a2b48 !important;}” el_id=”mobileshoecontact” el_class=”show-mobile”][vc_column_inner el_class=”whitecontent” width=”1/2″][vc_column_text css=”.vc_custom_1646218780746{margin-bottom: 0px !important;}”]

+91 9693339999

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][vc_column_inner el_class=”whitecontent” width=”1/2″ css=”.vc_custom_1644654313324{border-left-width: 1px !important;border-left-color: #ffffff !important;border-left-style: solid !important;}”][vc_column_text css=”.vc_custom_1646218770852{margin-bottom: 0px !important;}”]

Book Your Packages

[/vc_column_text]

[/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_custom_heading text=”Solang Valley” font_container=”tag:h4|text_align:left”][vc_column_text]

The Solang Valley has located 13 km from Manali and is famous for its glaciers and snow-capped mountains. During the winter, people visit the Valley to enjoy skiing. And in summer, the place is famous for zorbing and paragliding. Some of the other things you can do at Solang Valley include camping, quad biking, and snowmobiling.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_custom_heading text=”Beas Kund” font_container=”tag:h4|text_align:left”][vc_column_text]

The Beas River is one of the five major rivers in North India, giving Punjab its name. Beas Kund is believed to be the spot where the river originates. It is a popular trekking destination thanks to the lush green grasslands and the panoramic view offered by the Pir Panjal Range. The place also has historical significance – it is believed to be where Sage Vyas took a bath every day.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_custom_heading text=”Hidimba Devi Temple” font_container=”tag:h4|text_align:left”][vc_column_text]

The Hidimba Devi Temple is dedicated by Hidimba Devi, wife of Bheem, one of the Pancha Pandavas from the Mahabharatha. It is also locally called the Dhungari Temple, thanks to the Dhungiri Van Vihar, a cedar forest surrounding the temple. The temple is over 500 years old; it does not have an idol but a footprint on a stone.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_single_image image=”19386″ img_size=”full” alignment=”center”][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_custom_heading text=”Jogini Falls” font_container=”tag:h4|text_align:left”][vc_column_text]

Jogini Falls is about 3 km from Manali and is a popular trekking route covering the Beas River. It is frequented by locals who visit the Jogini Mata Temple close to the waterfalls. Tourists love the beautiful apple orchards and pine trees that dot the way.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_custom_heading text=”Rohtang Pass” font_container=”tag:h4|text_align:left”][vc_column_text]

The Rohtang Pass lies at an altitude of 3,978 meters above sea level and connects the valleys of Manali and Lahaul. It is only accessible from July to September and is closed during winters due to the extreme weather.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_custom_heading text=”Van Vihar” font_container=”tag:h4|text_align:left”][vc_column_text]

The Dhungri Van Vihar is a cedar forest located behind the Hidimba Temple. It is the perfect location for young couples looking for some solitude amidst the tourist crowd of Manali. The park opens at 8 am and is open until 5 pm, with the entry fee being Rs. 5 only.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_custom_heading text=”Gulaba” font_container=”tag:h4|text_align:left”][vc_column_text]

Gulaba is a historical village located on Rohtang Pass on the Leh -Manali Highway. This quaint place lies at an altitude of 4000m and is famous for its breathtaking view of the surrounding snowcapped mountains—the name origins from Raja Gulab Singh of Kashmir, who was on his way to invade China.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_custom_heading text=”Best Waterfalls in Manali” font_container=”tag:h3|text_align:left”][vc_column_text]

If you have already decided on our romantic Manali honeymoon tour packages, you should visit the following impressive waterfalls. Some of the most famous ones include:

  • Jogini Falls
  • Rahala Waterfalls
  • Jana Waterfall
  • Rozy Waterfalls
  • Knoi Waterfall

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_single_image image=”19388″ img_size=”full” alignment=”center”][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_custom_heading text=”Which Is the Best Time to Visit Manali for Honeymoon?” font_container=”tag:h3|text_align:left”][vc_column_text]

Manali is beautiful throughout the year, but it is pretty crowded during the summer months. October to June is also an ideal time to visit, thanks to the pleasant climate and colorful flowers that bloom in this season. If you’re looking for Manali honeymoon packages that include winter-based activities, December to February are the best season. Book one from July to September if you’re looking for budget Manali honeymoon packages for couples. It rains in Manali during this time of the year, making it unsuitable for sightseeing and adventure activities.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_custom_heading text=”Why Is ATW Travels the Best Travel Agency for Manali Honeymoon Packages?” font_container=”tag:h3|text_align:left”][vc_column_text]

ATW Travels offers the best Manali honeymoon packages for couples. Here’s why you must choose us to make your honeymoon memorable.

  • Customized tour packages
  • All safety protocols and COVID norms followed
  • Itinerary covers all major destinations
  • 24×7 customer service available
  • All-inclusive packages

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_custom_heading text=”Best Hotels and Resorts to Stay in Manali for Honeymoon” font_container=”tag:h3|text_align:left”][vc_column_text]

Here is the list of budget-friendly hotels and resorts to stay in Manali for your honeymoon.

  • The Orchard Greens
  • Welcome Heritage Urvashi’s Retreat
  • Snow Valley Resorts
  • Sun Park Resort Manali
  • Renest River Country Resort Manali
  • Amara Resorts
  • The Orchard Greens
  • Apple Country Resort
  • Mastiff Grand Manali
  • Manuallaya -The Resort Spa in the Himalayas

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_column_text]

Follow our Instagram profile to know about the latest offers and discounts.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner css=”.vc_custom_1645771843064{padding-right: 0px !important;}”][vc_column_text]

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_empty_space height=”20px” css=”.vc_custom_1646647405109{margin-bottom: 0px !important;}”]

FAQs about Manali Honeymoon

What Is the Best Time to Honeymoon in Manali?
Manali is pretty much busy during the summer months, so couples can visit during the winter months from December to March to enjoy privacy.
How Many Days Are Enough for The Manali Trip?
We recommend the 8 nights – 9 days Manali honeymoon package if you’d like to include all popular destinations in your itinerary.
Is Manali Safe for Honeymoon Couples?
Yes, Manali is safe for young couples. However, we request you follow safety instructions directed by your local guide and travel agent.
How To Visit Manali?
To reach Manali, you can opt for a flight - the Kullu Manali Airport is nearest to Manali, located 50 kilometers away from the town. The nearest railway station to Manali is Joginder Nagar, connected to several key cities around the country. Ambala and Chandigarh are two other rail stations.
How Can I Travel Within Manali?
While there are local buses within Manali, they aren’t connected to all popular tourist destinations. Besides, most buses are crowded and not very comfortable. You can travel by taxi or shared cab. Luckily, our Manali honeymoon package includes road transfers as well, allowing you to enjoy a hassle-free vacation.
What Are the Visa Requirements for International Travelers Visiting Manali?
International travelers must carry their passports and a valid visa to travel in and around Manali.
